title: Homemade Cooking Chorizo
summary: Level up your Mexican cooking by making your own chorizo. It’s delicious, easy to make and will completely transform your Mexican dishes.

method.description: Remove the stems and seeds from the dried guajillos and ancho chillies. Place the chillies in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Set aside for around 10 mins to rehydrate.

Once soft and hydrated, remove the chillies from the water and place them in a high-speed blender along with the vinegar, all the spices, vegetable oil and salt. Blitz well until smooth (this is the adobo for the chorizo).
Pass the adobo through a sieve to remove any bits that did not blend properly.
In a large bowl, mix the minced pork and the adobo until all the meat is well combined with the adobo.

Leave to rest for at least 2 hrs before cooking with it. You can store the chorizo in a plastic Tupperware container, in sandwich bags or in sausage cases. Or, divide it into small sausage-sized pieces, wrap them in cling film then wrap them again in tin foil or pop in a sandwich bag and store in the freezer. The chorizo will keep in the fridge for up to 4 days or freezer for up to 3 months.

chefs.name: Karla Zazueta
chefs.summary: Karla Zazueta is an incredible Mexican cook, food writer and cookery teacher. She teaches her Mexican traditions, recipes and techniques through cooking classes at her Mexican Food Memories cooking school. She also hosts Mexican supper clubs at her family home. Her recipes have been featured in The Guardian, BBC Food, NatGeo Food Magazine and many more. She's also just released her first cookbook, Norteña, where she shares the story of her family recipes and northern Mexico.
